kernels.name: gemv_q4k
kernels.why: Q4_K dense GEMV, ~42% BW vs llama 64% — ALU/latency-bound, not at the BW ceiling. The dense-decode lever: port the SoA int-dot path (gemv_q6_soa) to Q4_K.

kernels.name: gemv_q6_soa
kernels.why: Q6_K SoA int-dot GEMV — at ~57% BW, the good path. Near the memory roofline; little headroom.

kernels.name: gemv_q8_0_soa
kernels.why: Q8_0 SoA GEMV for a handful of projections — bandwidth-bound, small share.

kernels.name: fa2_partial
kernels.why: FlashAttention-2 partial (decode T==1 attention) — compute/ALU-bound at short context.

kernels.name: rms_norm
kernels.why: RMSNorm — memory-bound elementwise, fused where possible.

kernels.name: rope_neox
kernels.why: NeoX RoPE apply on Q/K — small bandwidth-bound op.

kernels.name: softmax_partial
kernels.why: Partial softmax reduction inside FA2 — compute-bound.

kernels.name: kv_append
kernels.why: KV-cache append (1 token) — tiny kernel, dominated by launch/submit overhead.

kernels.name: add_residual
kernels.why: Residual add — sub-launch-overhead op; could fuse into the preceding GEMV.
